fyPerformance: FY26 total income stood at ₹4,509 Cr (+20% YoY), while income excluding Noida grew 17%

fyPerformance: India operations revenue grew 10% YoY
India hospital EBITDA margin improved meaningfully in Q4FY26
India hospital margin stood around 25% in Q4FY26 vs around 22% last year
UK acquisition caused near-term margin dilution due to one-time acquisition costs
Normalized group margin stood around 22% after adjusting UK acquisition cost impact
Narayana Hrudayalaya Ltd.outlook: Management is building an integrated care and global healthcare platform, while UK integration, insurance loss moderation and clinic cash burn remain key monitorables.
highlights: India margin improvement was driven by higher-end procedures, better case mix, robotics and transplant work
Management uses ARPIP and throughput as better operating measures than only occupancy / ARPOB
Occupancy typically operates around 60-65%
Clinics act as feeders for inpatient referrals and advanced therapies
Cayman insurance has scaled faster than expected with annualized premium run-rate around $60 Mn
No additional capex is planned in Cayman currently
Narayana Hrudayalaya Ltd.quote: "Narayana is outperforming the sector on India hospital margin improvement, technology-led efficiency, integrated care model and global healthcare platform build-out."
